rulings.Case Title: DAVID P. DINSLAGE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Tax And/Or Strike Claimed Costs Sought By Deft City And County Of San Francisco
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 14, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF DAVID DINSLAGE's Motion To Tax And/Or Strike Claimed Costs Sought By Defendant City And County Of San Francisco. Judge Miller discloses and will disclose at the hearing that she is social friends with Deputy City Attorney Elizabeth Salveson. Judge Miller practiced law in the same firm as Ms Salveson from December 1981 - March 1987, and from 1992 through 1995. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this matter she she does not believe that a person aware of these facts would reasonably have a doubt that I would be able to be impartial. The motion to tax and/or strike claimed costs is granted in part and denied in part. Denied as to the filing fees because a public entity is entitled to recover fees incurred, even if not paid. (Guillemin v. Stein (2002) 104 Cal.App.4th 156, 164.) Denied as to the process server's fee for serving the subpoena to DFEH because Plaintiff withdrew his request to tax this cost in his reply brief. (Plaintiff's reply brief, p. 3.) Granted as to the messenger fees in the amount of $205.00. Defendant has not established that the use of the messenger service was "reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient or beneficial to its preparation." (Benach v. County of Los Angeles (2007) 149 Cal.App.4th 836, 857.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA SUZANNE FARLEY VS. MARTHA LYDDON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 14, 2014, Line 11, PETITIONERS BARBARA FARLEY, and CHARLES WISCH's Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award. The court grants the petition to confirm the arbitration award. Respondent concedes that she was served with the award and the notice of her rights, but she failed to file a timely objection to the award. She seeks relief from her failure to reject the award and move for trial within the 30-day time limit permitted by law under the Mandatory Fee Arbitration Act ("MFAA") (See Business & Professions Code section 6203(b)). However, Respondent cannot obtain relief from this time limit pursuant to CCP 473. See Maynard v. Brandon (2005) 36 Cal.4th 364, 382 ("[w]e conclude that the availability of section 473(b) relief in connection with judicial arbitration does not alter the inflexible nature of the 30-day time limit for seeking a trial following fee arbitration under the MFAA"). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: OSCAR VALLADARES VS. SCOTT MATTHEW CACURAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Re-Open Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 14, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT SCOTT CACURAK Motion To Re-Open Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Dennis Hyde, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Tentatively, the unopposed motion to re-open discovery would be granted to allow the specific "minimal" discovery that is sought. However, moving party must specify the particular discovery that is sought. Moving party should be prepared to provide the expert witness disclosure to the Court for consideration. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing.

rulings.Case Title: KENTON WONG VS. CSTV ONLINE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 14, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT CSTV ONLINE, INC.'s MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Motion to Set Aside Default is granted. Defendant was diligent in filing the Motion for relief, the Motion was accompanied by an attorney declaration attesting that attorney mistake and neglect caused entry of default, and there is no basis for the Court to find otherwise. Thus, relief is required and the Motion should be granted. (CCP § 473(b).) rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY A. JOHNSON VS. ASHFIELD CAPITAL PARTNERS LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adj; Index
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 14, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT ASHFIELD CAPITAL PARTNERS LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication; Index. (pat 1 of 2 of tentative ruling) Motion for summary judgment granted. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is not barred by CCP sec. 437(c)(f)(2) because it is based on legal theories and issues not raised in the previous motion. (Nieto v. Blue Shield (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 60, 72; Patterson v. Sacramento City Unified School District (2007) 155 Cal.App.4th 821, 827.) As to the second cause of action for Retaliation in Violation of Public Policy, Defendant met its initial burden by showing that Plaintiff cannot establish that Plaintiff disclosed information to a government or law enforcement agency, or that he refused to participate in an illegal activity, as required by Labor Code section 1102.5 that was operative at the time of Plaintiff's termination. (Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 9-34.) Plaintiff has not raised a triable issue of material fact as to whether he disclosed information to a government or law enforcement agency, as his deposition testimony contradicts the non-specific statement in his declaration (at paragraph 26) submitted in opposition. (See D'Amico v. Board of Medical Examiners (1974) 11 Cal.3d 1, 21-22.) Further, Plaintiff has not raised a triable issue of material fact as to whether he refused to participate in illegal activity. Plaintiff does not dispute that he participated in the initial purchase of stocks with the AZS system and introduces no evidence that after the initial incident Plaintiff was asked to continue to purchase stocks with the AZS system. Moreover, Plaintiff fails to identify any specific statute or regulation that was violated by using the AZS system to purchase stocks. As to the third cause of action for Retaliation in Violation of Labor Code section 98.6, Plaintiff asserts that this claim is based on his Lab. Code sec. 1102.5 claim. (Plaintiff's opposition brief, p. 8.) Because Plaintiff has not raised a triable issue of material fact as to the second cause of action, the third cause of action fails as a result. As to the first cause of action for Wrongful Termination in Violation of Public Policy, Plaintiff asserts that the public policy violated was Lab. Code sec. 1102.5 (Plaintiff's opposition brief, p. 7-8.) Because Plaintiff has not raised a triable issue of material fact as to the second cause of action for retaliation, this cause of action also fails. Plaintiff has not identified any other specific statute or Constitutional provision that was violated. (See Turner v. Anheuser-Busch (1994) 7 Cal.4th 1238, 1256.) As to the causes of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress and Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 17200, because Plaintiff has not raised a triable issue of material fact as to whether he was terminated in violation of public policy or in violation of the Labor Code, these causes of action also fail. The Court grants Plaintiff's request for judicial notice as to items 4, 5, 6, 13, 14, and 15. The balance of the request for judicial notice is denied for failure to comply with Rule of Court 3.1306(c). As to the federal statutes or regulation for which judicial notice is requested (e.g., items 9-12), Plaintiff has not even provided a citation to the U.S. Code or the Code of Federal Regulations to enable the Court to locate the statute, let alone the particular section or regulation for which plaintiff seeks judicial notice. (end of part 1 of 2 of tentative ruling) =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL HANZEK VS. PATRICK MALKASSIAN et al

rulings.Case Title: JUSTIN MOORE VS. RICHARD BURDEN TEED
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 14, 2014, Line 7, CROSS DEFENDANT DONALD MOORE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e).) "[U]nless the prospective indemnitor and indemnitee are jointly and severally liable to the plaintiff there is no basis for indemnity" (Yamaha Motor Corp. v. Paseman (1990) 219 Cal.App. 958, 964), and "there can be no indemnity without liability" (Western Steamship Lines, Inc. v. San Pedro Peninsula Hospital (1994) 8 Cal.4th 100, 114.) The First Amended Cross-Complaint still does not plead facts establishing that Cross-Defendant could be held liable in tort to Plaintiff in the underlying action, and Cross-Plaintiffs' attempt in opposition to base tort liability on torts not alleged in the pleading cannot cure defects in that pleading. Thus, the First Amended Cross-Complaint does not state a cause of action for equitable indemnity or contribution. Furthermore, the theories of liability raised in opposition fail to coherently establish any liability on the part of Cross-Defendant, and leave to amend is denied. Cross-Defendant's request for judicial notice of court documents filed in this action is granted. Cross-Complainant's request for judicial notice in its Sur Reply/Notice of Request for Judicial Notice, filed August 11, 2014, is denied. Cross-Complainant's authority does not support a Court taking judicial notice of an opposing party's discovery responses in the context of ruling on a demurrer. (Cf. Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co. (1999) 21 Cal. 4th 71, 83 ("complaint's allegations may be disregarded when they conflict with judicially noticed discovery responses").) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
